---
title: "Coastal Plain Hawkweed (Hieracium megacephalon)"
slug: "hieracium-megacephalon"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475148896/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Coastal Plain Hawkweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475148896/medium.jpg" alt="Coastal Plain Hawkweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/damien6868">
damien6868</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264603436">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=154591)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in dry to mesic sandy habitats.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its basal rosette of hairy leaves and tall stems bearing yellow, dandelion-like flower heads. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic sandy habitats such as flatwoods, sandhills, and open pine woodlands. It typically blooms during the warmer months and provides a nectar source for various pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](http://inaturalist.org/taxa/154591-Hieracium-megacephalon)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2492)
